session.notes: Session contains 9 slides and 3 sections after velocity_commit_deck.

scores.notes: Core statistics, deck export, and session export were correct; richer deck specs still exposed memory pressure.
The intended round-trip path was initially blocked by a missing deck-commit tool and later required a larger-heap workaround.
Topic discovery was usable, but demographic navigation still required manual fallback.
A coherent 9-slide deck and matching session file were produced.
Suggestions were shallow and did not steer around weight ambiguity or build-size risk.
The brief was useful, but the original MCP docs omitted the deck-commit step needed for session handoff.

notableFindings: Good or fair sleep accounts for 61.6% of valid responses, but 13.4% still report very poor or poor sleep.
Night waking rises from 65.1% in the youngest age group to 91.1% in the 51+ group (p = 0.000301047061425197).
Mean HADS anxiety rises from 4.71 in the best-sleep group to 8.25 in the worst-sleep group.
Among respondents with a sleep problem, energy level has the highest downstream impact mean at 6.48, ahead of mood and concentration.
blockers: MCP originally lacked a deck-commit step, so session export omitted built slides until velocity_commit_deck was added.
Richer 8-9 slide chart-heavy specs triggered default-heap OOM and then Invalid string length on higher-heap retry.
The final successful run required NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 on the MCP server.
followUps: Redesign buildDeck transport so large BuiltDeck payloads do not depend on giant JSON responses.
Keep velocity_commit_deck in the MCP contract and document it as mandatory before session export.
Improve semantic discovery for demographic/break-variable lookup.
Add safer defaults or warnings around body-weight false positives and build-size risk.
